stories.headline: Sara Duterte impeachment trial turns to fight over subpoenaing her bank and tax records
stories.summary: On the sixth day of Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial, the court heard oral arguments on the prosecution's request to subpoena the bank, AMLC and tax records of Duterte and her husband, Manases Carpio. Earlier, House prosecutor Chel Diokno presented Anti-Money Laundering Council data he said showed her financial transactions exceeded 3 billion pesos while she was a Davao City official, which the prosecution ties to allegations of unexplained wealth. Prosecutors also withdrew most of their grave-threats witnesses, keeping only NBI Director Melvin Matibag.
stories.context: Duterte faces several impeachment articles, including alleged misuse of confidential funds and unexplained wealth, and grave threats against President Marcos, the first lady and former House Speaker Martin Romualdez.
stories.watch_for: The impeachment court is set to vote on July 20 on whether to subpoena the financial records, with a simple majority of senator-judges present required. Prosecutors are due to begin presenting confidential-funds witnesses on July 22.

stories.headline: Detained senator Marcoleta transferred to Quezon City jail after doctors clear him
stories.summary: The Sandiganbayan's Third Division ordered Senator Rodante Marcoleta moved from hospital detention to the New Quezon City Jail in Barangay Payatas after doctors at the Philippine General Hospital found he had recovered from pneumonia and was fit for regular detention. He had been held at a police hospital since his July 6 arrest on a plunder charge. The anti-graft court inspected the jail facility before finalizing the transfer.
stories.context: Marcoleta, a party-list senator, is accused of failing to declare a 75-million-peso campaign donation; he has filed a motion to quash the charge and has not yet been arraigned.

stories.headline: Philippine stocks slip further as Middle East tensions weigh on markets
stories.summary: The Philippine Stock Exchange index fell 0.15%, or 9.70 points, to close at 6,256.02 on Tuesday, while the broader All Shares index dropped 0.09% to 3,379.94, as investors grew more cautious over escalating US-Iran hostilities and rising global oil prices. Trading volume was thin, with many investors staying on the sidelines, BusinessWorld reported.

stories.headline: Marcos inaugurates first phase of solar-battery project developers call world's largest
stories.summary: President Marcos inaugurated Phase 1 of the Meralco-backed MTerra Solar project in Gapan, Nueva Ecija, which developers describe as the world's largest integrated solar and battery storage facility, with 1,373 megawatts of solar capacity and 825 megawatts of battery storage now energized. Marcos said the project reflects the government's push to diversify the country's energy sources. The full project, spanning five towns in Nueva Ecija and Bulacan, is designed to reach 3,500 megawatts of solar capacity and 4,500 megawatt-hours of battery storage.
stories.context: Terra Solar Philippines Inc. and Meralco PowerGen Corp. developed the project; once complete, it is expected to supply electricity to about 2.4 million households and cut annual carbon emissions by roughly 4.3 million metric tons.
stories.watch_for: Phase 1 is about 91% complete, with full commercial operation targeted for August 2026, while Phase 2 is expected to be completed in 2027.

stories.headline: Magnitude 5.2 quake jolts off Sarangani Island, felt across South Cotabato and Davao
stories.summary: A magnitude 5.2 earthquake struck nine kilometers east of Sarangani Island in Davao Occidental at 9:15 p.m. Tuesday, at a depth of 10 kilometers, the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said. The strongest shaking, Intensity III, was recorded in Malungon, Sarangani, with lighter intensity felt in General Santos City, Tupi, Kidapawan City, Digos City and Santa Maria. No damage or injuries were immediately reported.
stories.context: Phivolcs said the quake was an aftershock of the magnitude 7.8 earthquake off Maasim, Sarangani on June 8 and the magnitude 6.5 quake off Balut Island on June 26.

stories.headline: Southwest monsoon brings rains to northern Luzon as no tropical cyclone remains active
stories.summary: As of July 15, no tropical cyclone was active within the Philippine Area of Responsibility, PAGASA said, after Tropical Storm Josie earlier weakened into a low-pressure area. The southwest monsoon, or habagat, is bringing isolated rains and thunderstorms over Ilocos Norte, Batanes and the Babuyan Islands, while the rest of the country can expect localized afternoon thunderstorms over the next 24 hours. Light to moderate winds and moderate coastal waters are forecast.

stories.headline: Monsoon rains and high tides force class suspensions across Luzon provinces
stories.summary: Classes were suspended in parts of Bulacan, Pampanga, Pangasinan and Isabela on Tuesday due to flooding and high tides linked to the southwest monsoon, as well as localized power outages, according to local government announcements compiled by GMA News. In Macabebe and Masantol, Pampanga, authorities cited flooding from monsoon rains combined with a 5.2-foot high tide and extended the suspension through July 16.

stories.headline: Military tightens security in Basilan ahead of BARMM gun ban and September elections
stories.summary: The Armed Forces' Western Mindanao Command intensified security operations against remaining armed groups in Basilan and nearby areas ahead of a region-wide gun ban taking effect Thursday, officials said, as the Bangsamoro region prepares for its first parliamentary elections on September 14. Security officials said remnants of the Abu Sayyaf Group and splinter factions of the Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters could be exploited by local political actors during the election period.
stories.context: The September 14 parliamentary polls mark a major test of BARMM's transition from decades of armed conflict toward democratic governance under the Bangsamoro Organic Law.
stories.watch_for: A region-wide gun ban takes effect Thursday, July 16, ahead of the September elections.

stories.headline: Government says OFW repatriation from Middle East continues, though pace slows
stories.summary: The Department of Migrant Workers said it has repatriated 10,580 overseas Filipino workers and their dependents from the Middle East so far amid the US-Israel conflict with Iran, including 50 people from Kuwait who arrived this week. DMW Undersecretary Felicitas Bay said repatriation requests have declined compared with the April-to-May peak but assistance remains available to those who need it.

stories.headline: US resumes naval blockade of Iran and strikes Hormuz targets as Gulf retaliation widens
stories.summary: US Central Command launched a fresh seven-hour wave of strikes on Iranian military targets near the Strait of Hormuz early July 15 and reinstated a naval blockade of Iran's ports, less than a month after both sides signed a de-escalation memorandum. Iran's Revolutionary Guard fired drones and missiles at US-linked sites in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan, wounding four Kuwaiti naval personnel, Al Jazeera reported. Iran reported more than 30 civilians killed and over 260 injured, and Tehran declared the memorandum void. Brent crude rose to about 86 dollars a barrel.
stories.context: The strikes mark a sharp escalation after a US-Iran de-escalation memorandum signed in June; Iran had attacked commercial tankers in the Strait of Hormuz in the preceding days.
stories.watch_for: Iran's Revolutionary Guard has threatened to halt regional energy exports and disrupt shipping through the Strait of Hormuz if the blockade continues.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon resume Rome talks on troop withdrawal from south Lebanon
stories.summary: Lebanese and Israeli delegations met at the US embassy in Rome for a sixth round of talks aimed at implementing a US-brokered framework agreement to end their conflict, focused on a phased Israeli troop withdrawal from two areas of southern Lebanon. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun instructed his delegation to demand an immediate start to the Israeli pullout before further discussion, while Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Saar said Israel was ready to move forward with the withdrawal plan.
stories.context: Israel and Hezbollah reached the framework agreement in Washington on June 26, following renewed fighting that began March 2; the renewed US-Iran hostilities in the Gulf have raised concern the agreement could unravel.
stories.watch_for: The talks were scheduled to continue over two days, July 14-15.

stories.headline: Houthis strike Saudi airport with missiles and drones, declare 2022 truce over
stories.summary: Yemen's Houthi movement said it fired ballistic missiles and drones at Saudi Arabia's Abha International Airport, which the Saudi-led coalition said it intercepted, in retaliation for a strike a day earlier on Sanaa International Airport meant to block a plane carrying a Houthi delegation from Iran. A Houthi spokesperson said the group considers the informal de-escalation in place since 2022 to be over and warned it could disrupt shipping through the Bab al-Mandeb strait if the blockade on Sanaa's airport continues. No casualties were reported from either strike.

stories.headline: ICTSI becomes first Philippine company to reach 2-trillion-peso market value
stories.summary: International Container Terminal Services Inc., the port operator led by Enrique Razon Jr., became the first Philippine company to reach a market capitalization of about 2.02 trillion pesos after its shares closed at a record 999 pesos, GMA News reported. The company crossed the 1-trillion-peso mark less than 10 months earlier, in September 2025. ICTSI, which runs 33 terminals in 19 countries, reported first-quarter net income up 21 percent to 314.69 million dollars on revenue up 29 percent to 961.11 million dollars.

stories.headline: Metro Manila minimum wage to rise by 85 pesos in two stages, labor department says
stories.summary: The Department of Labor and Employment said the 85-peso increase in Metro Manila's daily minimum wage under Wage Order No. 27 will take effect in two tranches: 60 pesos on July 25, 2026, and 25 pesos on January 20, 2027. Once fully in place, the minimum wage rises to 780 pesos a day for most workers and 743 pesos for agriculture and small establishments. Labor Secretary Francis Tolentino described it as the largest single daily wage adjustment enacted in Metro Manila.

stories.headline: Peso rebounds from near-record low after softer US inflation data
stories.summary: The peso closed at 61.686 to the US dollar on July 15, strengthening 2.4 centavos from the previous day's near-record low, after softer-than-expected US inflation data eased expectations of another Federal Reserve rate increase, GMA News reported. US consumer prices rose 0.4 percent in June, down from 0.5 percent in May. RCBC chief economist Michael Ricafort said the dollar corrected against major currencies following the data.

stories.headline: Magnitude 6.5 earthquake strikes off Davao Occidental; no immediate damage reported
stories.summary: A magnitude 6.5 earthquake struck 104 kilometers southwest of Balut Island in Sarangani, Davao Occidental, at 11:49 p.m. on July 14, at a depth of 10 kilometers, the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said. The strongest shaking, Intensity V, was felt in Kiamba, Sarangani, with lower intensities recorded across South Cotabato, General Santos City and nearby areas. Phivolcs reported no immediate damage and warned of possible aftershocks; no tsunami warning was issued.
stories.context: The waters off southern Davao Occidental and Sarangani have seen repeated offshore earthquakes since a magnitude 7.8 quake near Sarangani on June 8.

stories.headline: China's second-quarter GDP growth slows to 4.3 percent, weakest in over three years
stories.summary: China's economy grew 4.3 percent year-on-year in the second quarter, its slowest pace in more than three years, as weak domestic consumption weighed on activity, according to figures reported by Nikkei Asia. Industrial production rose 5.4 percent in the first half, led by robotics and semiconductors, and Nikkei said US trade tensions and Middle East turmoil had so far proved less damaging than feared.

stories.headline: Russian strikes on Odesa kill three as Ukraine reports hitting Russian ships in Black Sea
stories.summary: A Russian drone and missile attack on the southern port city of Odesa killed at least three people and wounded three others, Ukrainian officials said, in what they described as a fifth straight day of strikes on port infrastructure. Ukraine's drone force commander said its forces had struck 17 Russian oil tankers, two gas tankers and a tugboat, claims Al Jazeera said could not be independently verified. Russia's Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov called Ukraine's attacks on shipping terrorism.
